journal_entry.description: Borrowed $200,000 from bank at 6% annual interest, 3-year term, semi-annual payments
journal_entry.rationale: Cash is received (asset increase) from a bank loan (liability increase). The principal amount borrowed is $200,000. Interest terms affect future entries but not the initial loan disbursement. No tax applies to loan proceeds under Canadian tax law (borrowed funds are not income). Both tuples show count of 1, requiring one debit line and one credit line.

output.reason: Atlas Van Lines receives $200,000 in cash from the bank. Cash is an asset, and receiving cash increases the asset balance. This is 1 debit line: asset increase (cash).

debit_classifier.reason: Atlas Van Lines receives $200,000 in cash from the bank. Cash is an asset, and receiving cash increases the asset balance. This is 1 debit line: asset increase (cash).

output.reason: Atlas Van Lines borrowed $200,000 cash from the bank. On the credit side: the bank loan creates a liability (Loan Payable or Notes Payable) which is a liability increase. There is only one credit line: the liability increase from the loan.

credit_classifier.reason: Atlas Van Lines borrowed $200,000 cash from the bank. On the credit side: the bank loan creates a liability (Loan Payable or Notes Payable) which is a liability increase. There is only one credit line: the liability increase from the loan.
